title: I Am a Strange Loop
author: Douglas R. Hofstadter
themes: The self is a strange loop — a self-referential pattern that arises from the brain's activity
Consciousness is not a thing but a process, a pattern of self-reference
Gödel's incompleteness theorem provides a model for understanding self-reference in minds
The 'I' is an illusion — but a beautiful and necessary one
Other people can exist as patterns in your brain — we carry each other inside us
Death is real and devastating, but the patterns we create live on in others
excerpt: I am a strange loop. The 'I' that I experience as myself is not a thing but a pattern — a self-referential, tangled hierarchy that emerges from the activity of my brain. When I think about myself, I am creating a strange loop. And that loop is what I call 'me.'
